observations.text: Exceptional identification of the PM/Legal guarantee vs. best-effort contradiction — called out as 'FUNDAMENTAL CONFLICT' with three resolution options and a nuanced hybrid recommendation.
Clock-start ambiguity given its own dedicated escalation with three options and a clear recommendation favoring the customer perspective.
Wrong-address exception thoroughly analyzed — not just noted but escalated with a tiered adjudication recommendation.

Auto-refund vs. discretionary goodwill contradiction identified as a separate escalation from the main guarantee conflict, correctly noting that engineering cannot build both 'automatic' and 'discretionary'.
Nine business rules extracted (exceeding the 5 minimum), each with testable assertions and well-reasoned invariant justifications.
Six escalations covering contradictions, ambiguities, and missing context — each with options and recommendations, far exceeding the 2 minimum.
Confidence of 35 with detailed reasoning about why it's low, plus explicit high/low confidence area breakdowns and stated assumptions.
Edge cases are excellent — the driver-cancels-at-minute-115 overlap scenario and the midnight-spanning window show deep analytical thinking.
Rejection reasons correctly prevent premature implementation by blocking on the unresolved core contradiction.

All business rules marked invariant:false — the 10km radius and 4-star driver threshold could reasonably be argued as invariants (system eligibility constraints), though the justifications for non-invariant are defensible.
